title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%;opacity:0.85">
  <!-- Three overlapping circles = the three revolutions. Centered in the LOWER 2/3 of the canvas so the giant title can dominate the top half. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1620)">
    <!-- Cognitive Revolution (cyan-white) -->
    <circle cx="-280" cy="-120" r="560" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.55"/>
    <circle cx="-280" cy="-120" r="555" fill="none" stroke="#9fd6ff" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.35"/>
    <!-- Agricultural Revolution (ash) -->
    <circle cx="280" cy="-120" r="560" fill="none" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.45"/>
    <!-- Scientific Revolution (red-violet, the only chromatic edge) -->
    <circle cx="0" cy="320" r="560" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.85"/>
    <!-- Three small dots in the three intersection regions to imply the milestones -->
    <circle cx="-140" cy="-260" r="6" fill="#cccccc"/>
    <circle cx="140" cy="-260" r="6" fill="#cccccc"/>
    <circle cx="0" cy="100" r="8" fill="#fc1c46"/>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: Vast 520px uppercase title sits at the top — the title IS the cover, just like the original Sapiens design. The three revolutions (Cognitive / Agricultural / Scientific) collapse to a tight overlapping Venn in the lower 2/3 so the circles feel like an archeological imprint under the type, not a diagram competing with it. The Scientific Revolution's edge is the sole red line; cyan + ash are achromatic family. Pill 70,000 YR anchors the bottom-left.
